# Cluster: pricing (laptop)

## Findings

```
FINDING: FAIL
TITLE: No Reference Price Anchor
SECTION: price-anchoring
ELEMENT: h2
SOURCE: VISUAL
OBSERVATION: The price displays as a single number with no comparative anchor — no crossed-out MSRP, no category average, no "was/now" framing to contextualize the value.
RECOMMENDATION: Add a reference price (MSRP strikethrough or category-average comparison) directly adjacent to the current price so the visitor has an anchor against which to evaluate the deal.
REFERENCE: price-anchoring.md:Finding 2
PRIORITY: HIGH
**Why this matters:** Anchored prices convert measurably better than bare prices because the brain evaluates value comparatively, not absolutely.
↳ price-anchoring.md, Finding 2 (Fixture, 2024) [Gold]
```

```
FINDING: PARTIAL
TITLE: Price Prominence Near CTA
SECTION: price-display
ELEMENT: h2
SOURCE: VISUAL
OBSERVATION: The price is visible but renders at similar visual weight to surrounding copy — it is not the second-most-dominant element on the page after the CTA.
RECOMMENDATION: Increase price font weight and size so it forms a clear visual pair with the Add to Cart button.
REFERENCE: pricing-psychology.md:Finding 7
PRIORITY: MEDIUM
**Why this matters:** The price + CTA pair is the decision moment — when they are visually coupled, the purchase step feels confident rather than ambiguous.
↳ pricing-psychology.md, Finding 7 (Fixture, 2024) [Silver]
```

## What's Working Well

- **price-display**: Price appears above the fold. ↳ pricing-psychology.md, Finding 1
